rationale: The cited paper appears only as part of general background claims that new products can be categorized as really new versus incrementally new products. In the citing paper, it is used in grouped citations to support this broad distinction, without discussion of the cited paper's core-versus-peripheral innovation locus theory, mechanisms, or specific findings. The citing study's hypotheses, measures, and process tests are developed mainly from other self-referencing and advertising-creativity sources rather than building directly on this specific article.
